title: Neumora discards depression asset, lays off staffers after pair of late-stage flops
snippet: Neumora Therapeutics is laying off 35% of workers after its most advanced asset failed a pair of Phase 3 studies, sending the biotech’s stock spiraling early Monday.

title: Germany rethinking drug price reforms after Lilly, Boehringer withdraw investments: Reuters
snippet: Weeks after Boehringer Ingelheim and Eli Lilly retracted billions of dollar in German commitments, the nation’s government is reportedly changing a contentious element of its planned healthcare reforms.

title: Elicio crashes on midstage pancreatic cancer miss but will advance to Phase 3
snippet: Elicio Therapeutics’ investigational cancer immunotherapy failed to meet the primary endpoint of disease-free survival in a Phase 2 trial—a result the company attributed mostly to a disproportionate number of patients with higher residual disease.

title: Sanofi wins type 1 diabetes nod for Tzield after requesting to revoke CNPV
snippet: Sanofi makes no mention of the Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher. Tzield was awarded the ticket in October 2025, but Sanofi requested withdrawal from the program after former CDER head Tracy Beth Høeg reportedly expressed skepticism of the drug.

title: Rhythm’s obesity drug scores ‘better than expected’ weight loss in rare genetic disease
snippet: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals’ Imcivree reduced fat—while boosting muscle—in patients with Prader-Willi syndrome.

title: Lilly sees quick return on $2.3B Ajax buy as blood cancer drug shows early efficacy
snippet: Eli Lilly’s new JAK2 inhibitor—which it obtained from the recent acquisition of Ajax Therapeutics—reduced spleen volume by more than a third in 70% of patients with myelofibrosis.

title: The commercial launch playbook: Team structure, readiness and execution
snippet: Intellia Therapeutics's Senior Vice President Maria Natale discusses why the most successful launches are shaped long before approval, with strategy, structure and patient insight at the core.

title: Despite FDA trial clearance, ibogaine metabolite won’t receive red carpet treatment
snippet: DemeRx is launching the first U.S. clinical trial of an ibogaine-derived drug candidate, marking a pivotal moment for a controversial psychedelic long sidelined by safety concerns.

title: Consummate chameleon Amylyx nears regulatory run in obesity surgery complication
snippet: After suffering the market withdrawal of its only product, Amylyx is gearing up for a pivotal Phase 3 readout in post-bariatric hypoglycemia. But the company's driving ethos is still to treat “debilitating, devastating” neurodegenerative diseases, co-CEO Justin Klee told BioSpace.

title: How does ibogaine work? Understanding matrix logic to make a safer drug
snippet: Ibogaine’s unconventional “matrix pharmacology” may underlie both its therapeutic promise and unpredictable cardiac risks. Unraveling this mechanism could help drug developers hoping to bring ibogaine analogs to market.

title: Genentech R&D cuts include longtime top exec
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: FDA imposes import alert on Indian plant after inspectors flag GMP failings
snippet: Officials sanctioned Dabur India months after FDA inspectors found bird droppings and data integrity deficiencies during an inspection of the plant.

title: Amgen shores up Tavneos’ FDA defense with Duke data analysis
snippet: After the FDA flagged patient deaths linked to Amgen's rare disease drug Tavneos and called for its voluntary removal, the pharma recruited an independent data analysis from Duke researchers to help build the case for the drug's continued market approval.

title: Novartis’ $12B Avidity buy pays dividends with Phase 1/2 muscular dystrophy win
snippet: The RNA-based medicine is one of a handful of antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates that Novartis acquired last October when it took over neuromuscular-focused Avidity Biosciences.

title: Novo hit with cybersecurity breach, urges vigilance among trial participants
snippet: Novo Nordisk said that the information affected by the breach shouldn’t allow third parties to “identify participants in our clinical trials” despite “unauthorized access” to patients’ personal data.

title: Beyond sunshine: Iberia’s biotech moment has arrived with developing capital networks
snippet: Strong science, lower costs and growing capital networks are putting Spain and Portugal on the biotech investment map, even as structural bottlenecks persist, according to two investors.

title: Genentech R&D layoffs hit top exec who’s been with the biotech nearly 30 years
snippet: The staffing changes will focus resources on work that creates the highest impact for patients, according to Genentech. The number of employees affected is unknown, but longtime veteran Vishva Dixit is out.

title: Sky-high Parabilis, Kailera IPOs spur optimism—but only for derisked biotechs
snippet: Biotechs are benefitting from the AI tech frenzy and inflation, but validated pipelines and careful planning are still key to the recent record-setting IPOs, experts say.

title: Takeda’s AI-designed pill bests BMS’ Sotyktu in head-to-head psoriasis trial
snippet: Takeda eyes an FDA run for its investigational psoriasis pill after the drug elicited total skin clearance in more than 35% of patients at 16 weeks—more than 2.5 times that in controls taking Bristol Myers Squibb’s Sotyktu.

title: Kardigan stitches up $369M IPO plan
snippet: Analysts are cautiously optimistic about an IPO rebound for biopharma. BioSpace is keeping track of companies that seek to trade on the public markets this year.

title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.
